article_meta.title: San Francisco sues 10 major food manufacturers over ultra-processe

reasoning: Generally neutral and attributes loaded claims to the lawsuit or industry groups. Minor slips include stating health impacts in disadvantaged communities and broad support from ‘experts and government agencies’ without clear attribution, but no systematic adoption of the article’s framing.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs and frames charged language as allegations (e.g., ‘alleges,’ ‘argue’).
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: States ‘leading to widespread health impacts’ and that ‘experts and government agencies support the connection’ without precise attribution; CDC citation doesn’t explicitly make that link.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: One or two assertions read as factual outcomes rather than claims of the suit.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Avoids dramatic rhetoric; ‘public health crisis’ is presented as an accusation, not as fact.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about motives beyond what the lawsuit alleges.
custom_reasoning.slant: Summarizes both the city’s allegations and industry pushback with comparable weight.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Includes city officials, industry groups, companies, experts, and CDC; minor generalization of ‘government agencies.’
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Subjective terms (‘deceptive,’ ‘addictive’) appear only within attributed allegations.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically neutral language; charged terms are clearly attributed.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balances allegations with counterarguments; no undue emphasis on negatives.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No deference or dismissal of any source category.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than source; only slight carryover via un-attributed health impact phrasing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Captures all major stances present; slight lack of specificity about the U.S. Health Secretary reference but overall complete.
